title: Reversible Flawed Merge Sort on Doubly Linked List

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Doubly linked list represented as an array of integers, where each node has references to the next and previous nodes.
statement.outputFormat: Whether or not the resulting linked list is sorted after the merge sort.
statement.notes: This problem needs to consider the reversibility of the linked list, meaning we must modify the merge sort function to work with doubly linked lists.

examples.explanation: The linked list 1 -> 2 is already sorted.
After sorting, the linked list should be 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5.

functionSignature.java: public boolean sortList(int[] head, int n);
functionSignature.python: bool sortList(self, head, n: int) -> bool:

solutions.python:
```python
class Solution:
  def sort_list(self, head, n):
    def merge_sort(h, t):
      if h is None or t is None or h.next is None or t.next is None:
        return
      slow = h
      fast = h
      pre = h
      while fast is not None and fast.next is not None:
        pre = slow
        slow = slow.next
        fast = fast.next.next
      mid = slow
      next_half = mid.next
      mid.next = None
      merge_sort(h, mid)
      merge_sort(mid.next, t)
      while pre.next is not None and next_half is not None:
        if pre.next.data > next_half.data:
          temp = pre.next
          pre.next = next_half
          next_half = next_half.next
          temp.next = mid
          mid = temp
        else:
          pre = pre.next
      if pre.next is not None:
        mid.next = pre.next
        pre.next = None
      head = h
    
    merge_sort(head, t)
    return True
   
# Define a Node class
class Node:
  def __init__(self, data):
    self.data = data
    self.next = None
    self.prev = None

# Driver code
if __name__ == '__main__':
  head = Node(4)
  temp = head
  temp.next = Node(2)
  temp = temp.next
  temp.prev = Node(1)
  temp = temp.prev
  temp.next = Node(5)
  temp = temp.next

  n = 5
  print(Solution().sort_list(head, n))
```
